message:
fix(fr-command,fr-runtime): FLUSHDB/FLUSHALL/LATENCY error parity [bd-frankenredis-2juo]

Three small wire-format divergences from vendored Redis 7.2.4:

- FLUSHDB / FLUSHALL strict argument validation. Upstream
  db.c::flushCommandCommon only accepts ASYNC and SYNC as the
  optional flag and emits 'ERR syntax error' for anything else
  (or for any argv > 2). fr was returning 'ERR wrong arity' on
  >2 args and silently treating any unknown 1-arg flag as ASYNC.
  Fixed in both fr-command::flushdb / fr-command::flushall (the
  fr-command dispatch path) and fr-runtime::handle_flushdb_command
  (the runtime fast path).

- LATENCY GRAPH on an event with no samples now emits the upstream
  'ERR No samples available...' wording. Previously the 'ERR'
  prefix was missing because the helper emitted the body via
  RespFrame::Error(<no-prefix string>) — addReplyError in
  upstream prepends ERR transparently.

- LATENCY DOCTOR now mirrors upstream's HAL-style "I'm sorry,
  Dave, I can't do that. Latency monitoring is disabled in this
  Redis instance..." message when latency-monitor-threshold == 0
  (the default fr state). Previously it emitted a short canned
  "Be happy!" message for all conditions. The empty-threshold
  branch and the no-reports-with-threshold-set branch are now
  both distinguished, matching upstream behavior.

Updates 3 conformance fixture cases (latency_graph_no_samples,
flushdb_wrong_arity in core_connection + core_server,
flushall_wrong_arity in both) to the new upstream-aligned wording.
The fr-command latency_graph_and_doctor_use_real_tracker_state
test is realigned and now also asserts the HAL-style disabled
message when threshold == 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
